title: Berlin Train Stations - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin train stations are spread across the city.** Do not assume every long-distance train uses Hauptbahnhof.
**For most first-time visitors, Hauptbahnhof is the easiest all-round arrival.** It has S-Bahn, U5, buses, trams, taxis, service points and a simple link toward Alexanderplatz.
**Südkreuz is the cleanest airport-side station.** Current BER guidance lists the FEX route via Hauptbahnhof, Potsdamer Platz and Südkreuz, with Südkreuz to BER at about 14 minutes before buffers.
**Ostbahnhof is useful for Friedrichshain and the east-west S-Bahn spine.** It is not the same thing as being at Museum Island or the World Clock.
**Gesundbrunnen and Spandau are good when your hotel or onward route is north or west.** If your first plan is central Berlin, add a real transfer buffer.
**Use the station by your next move, not by its name.** Luggage, a late arrival, a BER flight or a fixed tour meeting time should decide the first step.
